mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates applied to future cash flows, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or signal fails to beat baseline in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer confidence and construction activity, driving sustained positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1% monthly signals robust manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings, but this effect only materializes during expansionary phases of the business cycle.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 20-day returns during expansion phases or Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized reflects strong aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation acceleration above 0.4% triggers expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or inflation threshold shows no causal link to rate hike expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and unemployment rate decline indicates labor market tightening that raises wage pressures and compresses profit margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence signals yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real sales of existing homes exceeding 3% signals weakening consumer spending power, but this negative equity impact only occurs during recessionary or post-peak regimes.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 5-day returns during recessionary periods or fails to beat baseline in >60% of triggered instances.
